key_insight: Universal ML interatomic potentials can fail catastrophically in melt-quench amorphous simulations because accurate forces alone do not guarantee correct pressure-volume behavior under NPT conditions.
engineering_relevance: For practical amorphous materials design, robust validation must include energy-volume and pressure response checks, and the NVT-quench/NPT-equilibration workaround can produce realistic densities without extra ab initio training.
method: Benchmarked 8 leading uMLIPs on melt-quench generation of amorphous IrO2
Compared uMLIP dynamics against AIMD reference energies, forces, pressures, and densities
Tested two fixes: pressure-targeted fine-tuning and revised NVT-quench/NPT-equilibration
evidence_signal: All eight models produced severely underdense IrO2 (1-4 g/cm3 vs 10.04 g/cm3 AIMD), and across 30 materials the revised protocol reduced density MAE from 2.46 to 0.35 g/cm3.
watch_item: The revised protocol improves robustness, but uMLIPs still need explicit validation of pressure and equation-of-state behavior before use in non-equilibrium structural generation.
paper_title: Melt-Quench Failures and Practical Solutions for Universal Machine-Learning Interatomic Potentials in Amorphous Structure Generation
